what: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World Addresses
title: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World Addresses
text: Addresses validated time collection often heterogenously formatted, making difficult directly compare. goal addr clean, parse, standardize, match messy, real-world addresses R use data linkages. addr built around vctrs-based addr vectors, including addr() vector addr_number(), addr_street(), addr_place() subclass vectors.

title: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World Addresses
text: Install latest stable release addr R-universe : , install development version addr GitHub : Installing addr GitHub requires working Rust toolchain; install one using rustup.

title: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World Addresses
text: addr vectors behave like standard R vectors: recycle, subset, combine vctrs tooling. can parse text addr vector as_addr() build one subclass vectors addr().
code: addr_vec <- as_addr(c("3333 Burnet Ave Cincinnati OH 45229",                       "5130 Rapid Run Rd Cincinnati OH 45238"))

title: addr classes — addr_number
text: structures addr() addr_ classes derived subset United States Thoroughfare, Landmark, Postal Address Data Standard relevant residential, numbered thoroughfare addresses:   addr() combines addr_number(), addr_street(), addr_place() single addr vector:
code: Address    ├─ AddressNumber    │  ├─ AddressNumberPrefix    │  ├─ AddressNumber    │  ├─ AddressNumberSuffix    ├─ StreetName    │  ├─ StreetNamePreModifier    │  ├─ StreetNamePreDirectional    │  ├─ StreetNamePreType    │  ├─ StreetName    │  ├─ StreetNamePostType    │  └─ StreetNamePostDirectional    └─ Place       ├─ PlaceName       ├─ StateName       └─ ZipCode <addr>  @ number: <addr_number>  .. @ prefix  .. @ digits  .. @ suffix  @ street: <addr_street>  .. @ predirectional  .. @ premodifier  .. @ pretype  .. @ name  .. @ posttype  .. @ postdirectional  @ place : <addr_place>  .. @ name  .. @ state  .. @ zipcode

title: addr classes — addr_number
code: addr_number(   prefix = NA_character_,   digits = NA_character_,   suffix = NA_character_ )  addr_street(   predirectional = NA_character_,   premodifier = NA_character_,   pretype = NA_character_,   name = NA_character_,   posttype = NA_character_,   postdirectional = NA_character_,   map_posttype = TRUE,   map_directional = TRUE,   map_pretype = TRUE,   map_ordinal = TRUE )  addr_place(   name = NA_character_,   state = NA_character_,   zipcode = NA_character_,   map_state = TRUE )  addr(number = addr_number(), street = addr_street(), place = addr_place())

title: addr classes — addr_number
text: prefix (often fractional) appears digits digits primary street number address suffix (often letter/part) attached digits predirectional direction name premodifier descriptive modifier name pretype type/classification name name street name city/town/municipality name posttype type/classification name postdirectional direction name map_posttype logical; map posttype abbreviations? map_directional logical; map pre- post-directional abbreviations? map_pretype logical; map pretype abbreviations? map_ordinal logical; map ordinal street names abbreviations? state state territory abbreviation zipcode ZIP code (must five digits starting "000") map_state logical; map state abbreviations? number addr_number vector street addr_street vector place addr_place vector

title: addr classes — addr_number
text: addr, addr_number, addr_street, addr_place vector

title: addr classes — addr_number
text: field values must must character vector least length one (including missing values); length one fields recycled match length fields.

title: addr classes — addr_number
code: # define a new addr_number vector addr_number(digits = "290") #> <addr_number> function ()   #>  @ prefix: chr NA #>  @ digits: chr "290" #>  @ suffix: chr NA addr_number(prefix = "N", digits = "290", suffix = "A") #> <addr_number> function ()   #>  @ prefix: chr "N" #>  @ digits: chr "290" #>  @ suffix: chr "A"  # define a new addr_street vector addr_street(name = "Burnet", posttype = "Ave") #> <addr_street> function ()   #>  @ predirectional : chr NA #>  @ premodifier    : chr NA #>  @ pretype        : chr NA #>  @ name           : chr "Burnet" #>  @ posttype       : chr "Ave" #>  @ postdirectional: chr NA  # street names are automatically mapped to abbreviations addr_street(predirectional = "North", name = "Fifth", posttype = "Street") #> <addr_street> function ()   #>  @ predirectional : chr "N" #>  @ premodifier    : chr NA #>  @ pretype        : chr NA #>  @ name           : chr "5TH" #>  @ posttype       : chr "St" #>  @ postdirectional: chr NA  # define a new addr_place vector addr_place(name = "Cincinnati", state = "OH", zipcode = "45220") #> <addr_place> function ()   #>  @ name   : chr "Cincinnati" #>  @ state  : chr "OH" #>  @ zipcode: chr "45220"  # define a new addr vector addr(   addr_number(digits = "290"),   addr_street(name = "Burnet", posttype = "Ave"),   addr_place(name = "Cincinnati", state = "OH", zipcode = "45229") ) #> <addr> #>  @ number: <addr_number> function ()   #>  .. @ prefix: chr NA #>  .. @ digits: chr "290" #>  .. @ suffix: chr NA #>  @ street: <addr_street> function ()   #>  .. @ predirectional : chr NA #>  .. @ premodifier    : chr NA #>  .. @ pretype        : chr NA #>  .. @ name           : chr "Burnet" #>  .. @ posttype       : chr "Ave" #>  .. @ postdirectional: chr NA #>  @ place : <addr_place> function ()   #>  .. @ name   : chr "Cincinnati" #>  .. @ state  : chr "OH" #>  .. @ zipcode: chr "45229"  # define a more complicated addr vector # and explicltly specify empty components to avoid NA addr(   addr_number(prefix = "", digits = "200", suffix = ""),   addr_street(     predirectional = "west",     premodifier = "Old",     pretype = "US",     name = "50",     posttype = "avenue",     postdirectional = "east",     map_directional = TRUE,     map_pretype = TRUE,     map_posttype = TRUE   ),   addr_place(name = "Cincinnati", state = "ohio", zipcode = "45220") ) #> <addr> #>  @ number: <addr_number> function ()   #>  .. @ prefix: chr "" #>  .. @ digits: chr "200" #>  .. @ suffix: chr "" #>  @ street: <addr_street> function ()   #>  .. @ predirectional : chr "W" #>  .. @ premodifier    : chr "Old" #>  .. @ pretype        : chr "US Hwy" #>  .. @ name           : chr "50" #>  .. @ posttype       : chr "Ave" #>  .. @ postdirectional: chr "E" #>  @ place : <addr_place> function ()   #>  .. @ name   : chr "Cincinnati" #>  .. @ state  : chr "OH" #>  .. @ zipcode: chr "45220"  # addr_* vectors are recycled and omitted fields are missing addr(   addr_number(digits = c("290", "200", "3333", "111")),   addr_street(     name = c("Burnet", "Main", "Ludlow", "State Route 32"),     posttype = c("Ave", "St", "Ave", NA_character_)   ),   addr_place(name = "Cincinnati", state = "OH") ) #> <addr> #>  @ number: <addr_number> function ()   #>  .. @ prefix: chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  .. @ digits: chr [1:4] "290" "200" "3333" "111" #>  .. @ suffix: chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  @ street: <addr_street> function ()   #>  .. @ predirectional : chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  .. @ premodifier    : chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  .. @ pretype        : chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  .. @ name           : chr [1:4] "Burnet" "Main" "Ludlow" "State Route 32" #>  .. @ posttype       : chr [1:4] "Ave" "St" "Ave" NA #>  .. @ postdirectional: chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  @ place : <addr_place> function ()   #>  .. @ name   : chr [1:4] "Cincinnati" "Cincinnati" "Cincinnati" "Cincinnati" #>  .. @ state  : chr [1:4] "OH" "OH" "OH" "OH" #>  .. @ zipcode: chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA

what: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
text: convenience wrapper around addr fuzzy matching helpers returns left-join style result. addr columns matched index rows expanded one--many many--many matches.

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
code: addr_fuzzy_left_join(   x,   y,   by = "addr",   addr_fields = NULL,   suffix = c(".x", ".y") )

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
text: x, y data frames tibbles addr column addr column name x (y ); length-2 character vector c(x_col, y_col) addr_fields named vector osa_max_distances; max distances addr tag field present default used (see details). suffix character vector length 2 used suffix duplicate columns

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
text: data frame left-join semantics

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
text: addr_fuzzy_left_join works matching addresses grouped ZIP codes, specified osa_max_distances place fields ignored Defaults addr_fields: number_prefix: 0 number_digits: 0 number_suffix: 0 street_predirectional: 0 street_premodifier: 0 street_pretype: 0 street_name: 1 street_posttype: 0 street_postdirectional: 0

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
code: my_addr <-   tibble::tibble(address = voter_addresses()[1:1000],                  addr = as_addr(address),                  id = sprintf("id_%04d", seq_len(1000))) #> Warning: street name post type not mapped: la the_addr <- nad_example_data() addr_fuzzy_left_join(my_addr, the_addr, c("addr", "nad_addr")) #> 49 of 49 unique ZIP codes in x matched to one of 60 unique ZIP codes in y #> matching by zipcode ■■                                 2% |  ETA:  3m #> matching by zipcode ■■■                                6% |  ETA:  2m #> matching by zipcode ■■■■                              10% |  ETA:  1m #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■                             14% |  ETA:  1m #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■                           20% |  ETA:  1m #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■                         27% |  ETA: 48s #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■■■                       33% |  ETA: 45s #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■■■■                      37% |  ETA: 41s #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                    43% |  ETA: 35s #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                  49% |  ETA: 31s #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                57% |  ETA: 24s #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■             65% |  ETA: 19s #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■            71% |  ETA: 15s #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         82% |  ETA:  9s #> matching by zipcode ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■     92% |  ETA:  4s #> Warning: Multi-matches detected for 23 addr in x; #> More than one row of y will be returned once for each matching row in x #> # A tibble: 1,796 × 10 #>    address             addr  id    nad_addr.y subaddress uuid  date_update s2    #>    <chr>               <add> <chr> <addr>     <chr>      <chr> <date>      <s2_> #>  1 3359 QUEEN CITY AV… 3359… id_0… 3359 QUEE… NA         {E3A… 2025-03-30  -6.7… #>  2 5179 RIVERWATCH DR… 5179… id_0… 5179 RIVE… NA         {CDE… 2025-03-30  -6.7… #>  3 4724 GLENWAY AVE C… 4724… id_0… 4724 GLEN… NA         {EB2… 2025-03-30  -6.7… #>  4 2375 FAIRGREEN DR … 2375… id_0… 2375 FAIR… NA         {204… 2025-03-30  -6.7… #>  5 259 CALVERTON LN C… 259 … id_0…            NA         NA    NA              … #>  6 1034 FASHION AVE C… 1034… id_0… 1034 FASH… NA         {DDD… 2025-03-30  -6.7… #>  7 2720 QUEEN CITY AV… 2720… id_0… 2720 QUEE… NA         {D94… 2025-03-30  -6.7… #>  8 4450 CLOVERHILL TE… 4450… id_0… 4450 CLOV… NA         {55F… 2025-03-30  -6.7… #>  9 175 ANDERSON FERRY… 175 … id_0… 175 ANDER… NA         {715… 2025-03-30  -6.7… #> 10 5270 WILLNET DR CI… 5270… id_0… 5270 WILL… NA         {A79… 2025-03-30  -6.7… #> # ℹ 1,786 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: address_type <chr>, parcel_id <chr>

what: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
text: addr_fuzzy_match() matches two addr vectors using one address tag fuzzy_match_addr_field() matches two addr vectors using single address tag Distances address tags defined using optimized string alignment; see fuzzy_match() stringdist::stringdist() details.

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
code: addr_fuzzy_match(x, y, addr_fields = NULL)  fuzzy_match_addr_field(   x,   y,   addr_field = c("number_prefix", "number_digits", "number_suffix",     "street_predirectional", "street_premodifier", "street_pretype", "street_name",     "street_posttype", "street_postdirectional", "place_name", "place_state",     "place_zipcode"),   osa_max_dist = 0 )

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
text: x addr vector match y addr vector match addr_fields named vector osa_max_distances; max distances addr tag field present default used (see details). addr_field character name single addr field match osa_max_dist maximum optimized string alignment distance used threshold matching single addr field

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
text: list integer vectors representing position best matching address(es) y address x

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
text: Defaults addr_fields: number_prefix: 0 number_digits: 0 number_suffix: 0 street_predirectional: 0 street_premodifier: 0 street_pretype: 0 street_name: 1 street_posttype: 0 street_postdirectional: 0 place_name: 0 place_state: 0 place_zipcode: 0 fuzzy matching street_name, "phonetic_street_key" prefilter automatically used (see ?fuzzy_match).

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
code: x_addr <- as_addr(c("123 Main St.", "333 Burnet Ave", "3333 Foofy Ave")) y_addr <- as_addr(c("0000 Main Street", "3333 Burnet Avenue", "222 Burnet Ave"))  # no matches with defaults addr_fuzzy_match(x_addr, y_addr) #> [[1]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[2]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>   # match on osa_max_dist of 2 for the address number addr_fuzzy_match(x_addr, y_addr, addr_fields = c("number_digits" = 2)) #> [[1]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>   # ignore address number when matching addr_fuzzy_match(x_addr, y_addr, addr_fields = c("number_digits" = Inf)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 3 #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>    fuzzy_match_addr_field(   as_addr(c("123 Main St.", "3333 Burnet Ave", "3333 Foofy Ave")),   as_addr(c("0000 Main Street", "0000 Burnet Avenue", "222 Burnet Ave")),   addr_field = "street_name", osa_max_dist = 1 ) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 3 #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>   # empty address fields have an OSA distance of zero and always match fuzzy_match_addr_field(   as_addr(c("123 Main St.", "3333 Burnet Ave", "3333 Foofy Ave")),   as_addr(c("0000 Main Street", "0000 Burnet Avenue", "222 Burnet Ave")),   addr_field = "number_prefix" ) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 2 3 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 1 2 3 #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] 1 2 3 #>

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
text: Fuzzy match strings x y using optimized string alignment (ignoring capitalization).

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
text: x character vector match y character vector match osa_max_dist maximum OSA distance consider match; Inf special case avoids computing string distance returning y instead just best match(es) 'y` prefilter method used prefilter y computing osa distances fuzzy matching speed calculations; "none" nothing, "psk" removes addrs y phonetic_street_key() x

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
text: list integer vectors representing position best matching string(s) y string x

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
text: multiple strings y tied minimum osa distances string x, indices included return value.

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
code: my_names <-   c("Pinye", "Pine", "Oalck", "Sunset", "Riverbend", "Greenfild") the_names <-   c("Piney", "Pine", "Oak", "Cheshire", "Greenfield", "Maple", "Elm") matches <- fuzzy_match(my_names, the_names, osa_max_dist = 1) matches #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[4]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[5]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[6]] #> [1] 5 #>   lapply(matches, \(i) the_names[i]) #> [[1]] #> [1] "Piney" "Pine"  #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "Pine" #>  #> [[3]] #> character(0) #>  #> [[4]] #> character(0) #>  #> [[5]] #> character(0) #>  #> [[6]] #> [1] "Greenfield" #>   x <- as_addr(voter_addresses()[1:100])@street@name y <- unique(nad_example_data()$nad_addr@street@name) system.time(fuzzy_match(x, y)) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.603   0.005   0.207  # larger vectors see a speedup when using # phonetic_street_key as a prefilter # but may miss potential matches that are within # osa_max_dist of each other, but did not have # identical phonetic codes (e.g., "woolper" and "woopler") system.time(fuzzy_match(x, y, prefilter = "psk")) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.378   0.003   0.165

what: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
text: geocode_tiger() geocodes addr vectors using TIGER address range geometries downloaded census.gov specific county. geocoding precision level matching achieved address: range: precise; address matched street name ZIP code, number within one ranges; point locations interpolated geometry best match using street number street: less precise; address matched street name ZIP code, number within one ranges; point locations interpolated centroid matching range geometries unmatched: precision; address match street name ZIP code combinations; point locations <null feature> (equivalent NA)

title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
text: x addr vector county character string county identifier year character year tigris product offset number meters offset geocode street line

title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
text: s2_geography vector geocoded point locations; attribute ("geocode_precision") added factor levels range, street, unmatched.

title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
text: street number-name multiple range parity matches, best match chosen based smallest width ranges range closest midpoint.

title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
text: single addr_number y chosen addr_number x. exact matches (using .character) found, possible matches (within thresh OSA distance) searched y. multiple matches present y, best one selected based lowest absolute numeric difference @digits x; ties broken optimized string alignment (OSA) distances preferring lowest value sorted Lexicographic order digits preceding alphabetic characters.

title: Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
text: x, y addr_number vectors match osa_max_dist integer maximum OSA distance consider match

title: Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
text: addr_number vector, length x, best match y addr_number code x; best match found missing value returned (addr_number())

title: Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
text: addr_number objects missing @digits empty strings @prefix, @digits, @suffix matched returned missing instead

title: Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
code: x <- addr_number(    prefix = "",    digits = as.character(c(1, 10, 228, 11, 22, 22, 22, 10, 99897, NA)),    suffix = ""  )  y <- addr_number(   prefix = "",   digits = as.character(c(12, 11, 10, 22)),   suffix = "" )  match_addr_number(x, y) #> <addr_number> function ()   #>  @ prefix: chr [1:10] "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" NA NA #>  @ digits: chr [1:10] "10" "10" "22" "11" "22" "22" "22" "10" NA NA #>  @ suffix: chr [1:10] "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" NA NA  match_addr_number(x, y, osa_max_dist = 0L) #> <addr_number> function ()   #>  @ prefix: chr [1:10] NA "" NA "" "" "" "" "" NA NA #>  @ digits: chr [1:10] NA "10" NA "11" "22" "22" "22" "10" NA NA #>  @ suffix: chr [1:10] NA "" NA "" "" "" "" "" NA NA

title: Match ZIP codes — match_zipcodes
text: single ZIP code y chosen ZIP code x. default, exact matches found, common variants ZIP codes x searched y. multiple variants present y, best one selected based lowest absolute numeric difference ZIP code x; ties broken OSA string distances preferring minimum number.

title: Match ZIP codes — match_zipcodes
text: x, y character vectors ZIP codes match zip_variants logical; fuzzy match common variants x y? (e.g., changing 4th 5th digit)

title: Match ZIP codes — match_zipcodes
text: character vector, length x, best match y ZIP code x

title: National Address Database (NAD) — nad_read
text: Download NAD R user's data directory addr package read data R county state.

title: National Address Database (NAD) — nad_read
text: county character, length one; name county state character, length one; name state

title: National Address Database (NAD) — nad_read
text: U.S. Department Transportation partners address programs state, local, tribal governments compile authoritative data database. Find information : https://www.transportation.gov/gis/national-address-database NAD downloaded latest release transportation.gov data portal: https://data.transportation.gov/dataset/National-Address-Database-NAD-File-Geodatabase/yw36-suxr/about_data original schema, see https://www.transportation.gov/sites/dot.gov/files/2023-07/NAD_Schema_202304.pdf NAD distinguish empty missing address components. reading R, missing address components replaced empty string ("") except address number (digits), street name, ZIP code.

what: Convert street names into phonetic matching keys — phonetic_street_key
title: Convert street names into phonetic matching keys — phonetic_street_key
text: Ordinal street names (e.g., "11TH", "5TH") encoded zero-padded numeric identifiers special prefix, non-ordinal street names encoded using Soundex phonetic code (see ?stringdist::phonetic). Ordinal words (e.g., "Eleventh", "Fifth") detected converted automatically. phonetic key exactly four characters long.

title: Convert street names into phonetic matching keys — phonetic_street_key
Convert street names into phonetic matching keys — phonetic_street_key
text: x character vector

title: Convert street names into phonetic matching keys — phonetic_street_key
text: character vector

title: Convert street names into phonetic matching keys — phonetic_street_key
Tag US addresses — tag_usaddress
text: Addresses tagged using usaddress conditional random field rust port usaddress. Possible address labels include: AddressNumberPrefix AddressNumberSuffix AddressNumber BuildingName CornerOf IntersectionSeparator LandmarkName NotAddress OccupancyIdentifier OccupancyType PlaceName Recipient StateName StreetNamePostDirectional StreetNamePostType StreetNamePreDirectional StreetNamePreModifier StreetNamePreType StreetName SubaddressIdentifier SubaddressType USPSBoxGroupID USPSBoxGroupType USPSBoxID USPSBoxType ZipCode Find information definitions https://www.fgdc.gov/standards/projects/address-data

title: Tag US addresses — tag_usaddress
text: x character string addresses clean logical; clean address text clean_address_text() tagging?

title: Tag US addresses — tag_usaddress
text: list vectors named address tags

Example addresses — voter_addresses
text: voter_addresses data generated example character vector real-world addresses. addresses downloaded Hamilton County, Ohio voter registration database 2024-09-12. See inst/make_example_addresses.R details. AddressPreDirectional, AddressNumber, AddressStreet, AddressSuffix, CityName, "OH", AddressZip pasted together create 242,133 unique addresses registered voters Hamilton County, OH.

text: character vector
